mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive and above historical median.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 PAYEMS at 142,071k showing continued labor market strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative growth months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) for an extended period, equity risk premiums compress and investors rotate into equities seeking yield, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS stays in the zero lower bound regime, signaling continued monetary accommodation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.13%, consistent with post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% for 3+ months, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns because simultaneous strength in both demand channels signals robust economic momentum that supports corporate profit growth.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 RSAFS at 446,477M and HOUST at 1,174k units showing demand strength
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or instances where both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction, which compresses profit margins and reduces equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns when INDPRO contracts by more than 0.5% in a single month.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 INDPRO at 107.477 showing recent production levels
regime_hint: economic slowdown or supply shock regime
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.2% or t-test p > 0.20), or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected earnings and increased risk aversion. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansions where labor market deterioration signals imminent recession.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 UNRATE at 5.3% representing near-full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.1% or t-test p > 0.18), or Rank IC > -0.04 in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns because the joint signal of inflation and restrictive policy creates headwinds for equity multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 CPIAUCSL at 237.786 and July 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% showing low inflation and accommodative policy
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC for the inflation-rates interaction < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or instances where both CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS > 2.0% do not produce significantly negative 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarter-over-quarter basis above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when real GDP growth remains above the long-term trend.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2015 GDPC1 at 16,270.4B showing continued economic expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized do not produce significantly higher subsequent 60-day returns than slower growth quarters (t-test p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports profit margins and equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns because simultaneous strength in production capacity and labor availability enables firms to meet demand without margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 INDPRO at 107.477 and PAYEMS at 142,071k showing supply-labor coordination
regime_hint: expansion regime with balanced supply-labor dynamics
failure_condition: Rank IC for the supply-labor interaction < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or months where both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow month-over-month do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.14).
